color_map: column header green = AOM (group_0); column header red = AOM+Smoking (group_1). Heatmap cell color is a per-row z-scored abundance scale (blue=low -2.5, red=high 2.5), independent of the group color coding.
notes: Panel C heatmap lists 20 species names on the right; columns are individual samples grouped under green 'AOM' (group_0) and red 'AOM+Smoking' (group_1) headers. Visually, the first 10 rows (Eggerthella lenta through Bacillus velezensis) show blue/dark (low) cells under the AOM columns and red (high) cells under the AOM+Smoking columns -> increased in group_1; this is corroborated by panel D (qPCR validation showing Eggerthella lenta relative abundance is significantly higher in AOM+Smoking, p<0.01). The remaining 10 rows (Streptococcus suis through Libanicoccus massiliensis) show the reverse pattern (bright red cells under AOM, dark/blue under AOM+Smoking) -> decreased in group_1. Panels A (alpha diversity), B (PCoA/beta diversity), and E (correlation network) were ignored per instructions.
